title: U.S. Department of Energy Announces $52 Million in Projects to Advance Production of Purpose-Grown Energy Crops
content: The DOE's Bioenergy Technologies Office (BETO) has allocated $52 million to six university and industry projects to advance low-carbon energy crops like algae, switchgrass, and miscanthus, supporting biofuels, bioproducts, and a clean energy bioeconomy. These projects, spanning 18 states, aim to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, boost agricultural innovation, and contribute to the federal goal of producing 35 billion gallons of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) by 2050. The initiatives align with DOE's Clean Fuels and Products Shotâ„¢ and will be coordinated under the new Regional Biomass Resource Hub Initiative led by Idaho National Laboratory.
author: Analysts Account
